notes: RELABELED from tags=[] during fixture review: tree.txt (declared ground truth) lists 14 real .tsx files in packages/playground (App.tsx, main.tsx, graph-visualizer.tsx...) with genuine react+react-dom+reactflow deps in playground's package.json — real JSX UI usage, so react fires, monorepo-scoped to playground. The original label curated this evidence away, contradicting the visible tree. Remaining discriminations this fixture tests: (1) react glob must be SCOPED to packages/playground, not repo-wide — packages/core is a pure TS library; (2) core's custom non-React JSX runtime (esbuild jsxImportSource './jsx-runtime', state-machine DSL in .ts files) must NOT widen the react scope to core — UI is the operative word; (3) no db, no distributed anywhere (verified: no ORM/client, no payment/queue/webhook/messaging side effects). Test convention: packages/core/tests/**/*.test.ts — corpus default glob already matches; kept unchanged.

rules_notes.generators-are-domain-documentation: + packages/core/tests/composition/laws.property.test.ts — named colocated fast-check generators reused across properties
rules_notes.near-identical-examples-signal-an-unextr: + packages/core/tests/components/state-fragment.test.ts — runs of structurally-similar example it() cases
rules_notes.discriminated-union-over-flag-bag: + packages/core/core/types.ts — mixes string-literal discriminants with optional boolean flag pairs (isInitial/isFinal)
rules_notes.state-modeling-escalation: + packages/core/core/types.ts — core domain IS state/event/transition modeling
rules_notes.value-object-for-domain-primitive: - no money/email/quantity primitives; cost/count fields are structural graph metrics
rules_notes.mood-names-commands-facts: - 'events' are freeform string transition labels, not a command/fact tag taxonomy
rules_notes.parse-dont-validate-at-every-untyped-bou: - pure in-memory library; no fetch/JSON.parse/DB row/SDK ingress in included core
